{"key_event":"Post-Tiananmen tightening amid market reforms","reasoning":"The CCP-state emerged from 1989 with tighter political controls, then gained fiscal, administrative, coercive, and legitimacy resources through the 1994 tax reform, technocratic restructuring, growth, nationalism, and crackdowns such as Falun Gong. Civil society remained weak and easily suppressed, but marketization, migration, private business, village elections, commercial media, and early internet use modestly expanded non-state resources and networks outside the old danwei system.","society_change":0.3,"society_power":3.5,"state_change":0.5,"state_power":8.7}
